figures.caption: Figure 1: (a) Exact head benefit (%) vs. dimension (≥ 4 seeds): declines from +28% at d = 2 to 0% at d = 16. (b) neff at mid-flow (t = 0.56) vs. dimension: the kernel collapses to 1-NN over the same range. (c) Multi-head scaling on R8 Gaussian mixtures: the exponent α (from MMD2 ∼ m−α) increases with head count H; more heads (each in lower dk = dmodel/H dimensions) yield faster rates.

Figure 4: (a) ImageNet DINOv2+PCA: method comparison across d. The exact head (ON) is marginal at d ≤ 16 and destabilizes training at d = 64. (b) Spherical shells: the plug-in fails 6–11× despite healthy neff , confirming geometry mismatch.
Figure 5: Whitened ImageNet sufficiency test. (a) neff drops 12× with whitening. (b) Plug-in MMD2 is unchanged, confirming neff alone is not causal.

Figure 2: (a) On spherical shells at d = 8, the isotropic NW kernel collapses to 1-NN at late flow times, while learned heads maintain multi-neighbor smoothing with differentiated bandwidths. (b) On DINOv2+PCA ImageNet features (d = 16), the learned model outperforms the plug-in at small m; the plug-in catches up as m grows. Crossover near m ≈ 25.

Figure 10: Support-conditioned generation on four R2 families (m = 50). Columns: ground truth, support set, plug-in, learned ICFM. The plug-in places mass near support points; the learned model generates fresh samples matching the ground-truth density.

Figure 3: (a) Correlation between IP-Adapter attention weights and Gaussian NW kernel weights, measured across denoising. Both Spearman ρ and Pearson r increase monotonically, reaching ρ = 0.89 at the final step. (b) Fraction of attention heads with ρ > 0.9; over half of heads are NW-like by the end of denoising.

Figure 8: Per-head attention entropy at d = 8, H = 8 (layer 0). Heads specialize to different effective bandwidths: some maintain high entropy (broad smoothing) while others collapse to near-zero (sharp, nearest-neighbor-like).

Figure 9: IP-Adapter null-model analysis (SD 1.5, 50 images, 128 heads). (a) Per-head ρ at t = 1: mean 0.885 far exceeds permutation null. (b) CDF: 56% of heads above ρ = 0.9. (c) ρ vs. denoising timestep between permutation null and same-NN ceiling.
Figure 6: (a) R2 GMMs: learned maintains 2.4–5.5× advantage at all m. (b) Shells d = 8: αshells ≈ αGMM; the rate bottleneck is meta-learning, not task complexity.
